description.en: The GNOME Display Manager (GDM) is a program that manages graphical display servers and handles graphical user logins.

rationale.en: If a Graphical User Interface (GUI) is not required, it should be removed to reduce the attack surface of the system.

audit.en:
Run the following commands to uninstall `gdm3` and remove unused dependencies:  

Impact:  

Removing the GNOME Display manager will remove the Graphical User Interface (GUI) from the system.

```
# apt purge gdm3
# apt autoremove gdm3
```

remediation.en:
Run the following commands to uninstall `gdm3` and remove unused dependencies:  

Impact:  

Removing the GNOME Display manager will remove the Graphical User Interface (GUI) from the system.

```
# apt purge gdm3
# apt autoremove gdm3
```
